name: MUI (Material-UI)
description: Comprehensive React component library implementing Google's Material Design with 10+ years of development

description.value: Comprehensive React component library implementing Google's Material Design with 10+ years of development

active-maintenance.comment: Very active: 2,500+ contributors, regular releases (v7.3.6 in Dec 2024), maintained by full-time MUI team

initial-release.comment: First commit on August 18, 2014. Project has been in active development almost as long as React itself

github-stars.comment: 97.5k stars as of December 2024, one of the most popular React component libraries

framework.comment: React-only library, though MUI also offers separate Base UI (headless) and Joy UI (alternative design system)

styling-approach.comment: Uses Emotion as default styled engine (CSS-in-JS). Also supports CSS variables via cssVariables: true configuration

component-model.comment: Fully styled components implementing Material Design. Base UI package offers unstyled/headless alternatives

typescript.comment: Written in TypeScript (49.6% TS, 50.2% JS). Requires TypeScript 4.9+ for full type safety

ssr-compatible.comment: Designed from ground-up for SSR. Requires Emotion cache setup for proper CSS injection during server rendering

rsc-compatible.comment: React Server Components compatible via AppRouterCacheProvider for Next.js App Router (v13+)

design-system.comment: Implements Google's Material Design specification, though fully customizable via theming system

dark-mode.comment: Built-in light and dark mode support with system preference detection and manual switching

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming via createTheme() covering palette, typography, spacing, breakpoints, shadows, transitions, and component overrides

css-variables.comment: CSS custom properties support via cssVariables: true configuration, enabling dynamic theme switching without re-rendering

design-tokens.comment: Organized design tokens for palette, typography (font scales), spacing (8px base), shape (border radius), shadows, and transitions

rtl-support.comment: Built-in right-to-left language support through theming system

component-count.comment: ~57 distinct components across 8 categories (Inputs, Data Display, Feedback, Surfaces, Navigation, Layout, Lab, Utils). Additional advanced components in MUI X

text-input.comment: TextField component with variants (outlined, filled, standard) and support for password, email, search, etc.

textarea.comment: TextField with multiline prop transforms into Textarea Autosize. Also standalone TextareaAutosize component available

select.comment: Select component for single selection, integrated with TextField or standalone

multi-select.comment: Select supports multiple selection via multiple prop, returns array of selected values

combobox.comment: Autocomplete component with filtering, search, and keyboard navigation. WAI-ARIA compliant combobox pattern. Supports virtualization via react-window

checkbox.comment: Checkbox component with support for groups and indeterminate state

radio.comment: Radio and RadioGroup components for mutually exclusive selections with keyboard accessibility

switch-toggle.comment: Switch component for boolean toggle inputs

slider.comment: Slider component supporting single and dual-thumb range selection

number-input.comment: NumberField component (new) with increment/decrement buttons. Recommended over TextField type='number' for better usability

tag-input.comment: No dedicated tag input component. Can be built using Autocomplete with freeSolo and multiple props

pin-input.comment: No dedicated OTP/PIN input component with individual digit boxes

color-picker.comment: No built-in color picker component with custom UI

date-picker.comment: Date Picker available in MUI X (free MIT license). Uses date library adapters (dayjs, date-fns, luxon, moment)

time-picker.comment: Time Picker available in MUI X (free MIT license)

date-range-picker.comment: Date Range Picker available in MUI X (free MIT license)

file-upload.comment: No built-in file upload component with preview

dropzone.comment: No built-in drag-and-drop file upload component

rating-input.comment: Rating component for star ratings and similar input

input-groups.comment: Input adornments support combining inputs with icons, buttons, and text (prefix/suffix elements)

input-with-dropdown.comment: Can be composed using TextField with Select or InputAdornment with Autocomplete

split-button.comment: No dedicated split button component. Can be built using ButtonGroup with Menu

table.comment: Basic Table component (Table, TableHead, TableBody, TableRow, TableCell). Includes TablePagination and TableSortLabel utilities but requires manual implementation

data-table.comment: DataGrid in MUI X (Community version free MIT license) with sorting, filtering, pagination, row selection, and editing. Pro/Premium versions add advanced features

virtual-infinite-list.comment: No built-in virtualized list component. Autocomplete supports virtualization via react-window integration

tree-view.comment: Tree View in MUI X (Community version free MIT license) with expansion, selection, and label editing. Pro adds drag-and-drop, lazy loading, and virtualization

list-view.comment: List, ListItem, ListItemButton, ListItemIcon, ListItemText components for styled lists with nested support and virtualization

badge.comment: Badge component for status indicators on other elements

timeline.comment: Timeline component in Lab package for vertical timeline of events

calendar.comment: No standalone calendar component for month/week display views. Date Pickers in MUI X include calendar UI for selection only

alert.comment: Alert component for static banner messages with severity variants

toast-notification.comment: Snackbar component for temporary popup notifications with auto-dismiss, positioning, transitions, and Alert integration

progress-bar.comment: LinearProgress component for determinate and indeterminate horizontal progress bars

progress-circle.comment: CircularProgress component for determinate and indeterminate circular progress indicators

spinner-loader.comment: CircularProgress serves as indeterminate loading spinner

skeleton.comment: Skeleton component with text, circular, rectangular, and rounded variants. Supports pulsate/wave animations and dimension inference from children

modal-dialog.comment: Modal and Dialog components with focus trap, backdrop, keyboard escape, and ARIA support

drawer-sheet.comment: Drawer component supporting all anchor positions (left, right, top, bottom) and three variants (temporary, persistent, permanent). Includes SwipeableDrawer for mobile

popover.comment: Popover component built on Modal for displaying content anchored to elements with flexible positioning

tooltip.comment: Tooltip component for hover hint text

dropdown-menu.comment: Menu component for action menus on click/hover

context-menu.comment: Menu component supports right-click context menu via event handlers

command-palette.comment: No built-in keyboard-driven command palette (⌘K style)

tabs.comment: Tabs component for horizontal tab-based content switching

vertical-tabs.comment: Tabs component supports vertical orientation via orientation prop

breadcrumb.comment: Breadcrumbs component for hierarchical path navigation

pagination.comment: Pagination component for page navigation controls

stepper.comment: Stepper component supporting both horizontal and vertical orientations for multi-step processes

navigation-menu.comment: No dedicated navigation menu component with nested dropdowns. Can be built using AppBar, Menu, and List components

sidebar.comment: No dedicated sidebar component. Drawer (persistent/permanent variants) typically used for sidebar navigation

bottom-navigation.comment: BottomNavigation component for mobile-style bottom nav bar (3-5 destinations with icons and optional labels)

grid.comment: Grid component using CSS Flexbox (not CSS Grid) for responsive 12-column layouts. Does not support row spanning or auto-placement

stack.comment: Stack component for one-dimensional flex-based layouts (vertical or horizontal) with spacing and dividers

container.comment: Container component for centered max-width layouts with fluid and fixed modes

aspect-ratio.comment: No AspectRatio component in Material UI (available in Joy UI alternative design system)

collapsible.comment: Accordion component for collapsible content sections with WAI-ARIA support. Collapse transition component also available

splitter.comment: No built-in resizable split panes component

rich-text-editors.comment: mui-tiptap provides Tiptap integration with Material UI components. No other official integrations

code-editors.comment: No official code editor integrations documented

chart-libraries.comment: MUI X Charts (Community free MIT, Pro paid) with D3-based data manipulation. Includes Bar, Line, Area, Pie, Scatter, Sparkline, Gauge (free); Radar, Heatmap, Funnel, Pyramid, Sankey (Pro)

form-libraries.comment: Official integrations: react-hook-form-mui, formik-material-ui, mui-rff (React Final Form), @ui-schema/ds-material

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in transition components (Fade, Grow, Slide, Collapse, Zoom). No documented third-party animation library integrations

icon-libraries.comment: @mui/icons-material package includes 2,100+ Material Icons as SvgIcon components in 5 styles (Filled, Outlined, Rounded, Two-tone, Sharp)

date-libraries.comment: MUI X Date Pickers support multiple date library adapters: dayjs (demonstrated in docs), date-fns, luxon, moment via adapter packages

aria-compliant.comment: Components follow WAI-ARIA authoring practices. Autocomplete explicitly implements WAI-ARIA combobox pattern

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard support across interactive components including Home/End key handling, arrow navigation, and focus management

screen-reader-tested.comment: Components designed with screen reader compatibility. Modal/Dialog include proper ARIA labeling (aria-labelledby, aria-describedby)

focus-management.comment: Proper focus trapping in modals (with disableEnforceFocus option), focus restoration, and focus management across components

wcag-level.comment: WCAG compliance level not explicitly documented in official materials

accessibility-rating.comment: Good accessibility with ARIA compliance, keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and focus management. Well-documented a11y patterns but not explicitly WCAG AA certified

bundle-size-full.comment: 831.7 KB minified (181.4 KB gzipped) for full @mui/material package

bundle-size-core.comment: Not applicable - no separate core bundle. Tree-shaking supported via path imports (@mui/material/Button) reduces bundle size significantly in production

tree-shakeable.comment: Modern bundlers tree-shake unused code in production. Use path imports (@mui/material/Button) instead of named imports for better development performance

zero-runtime.comment: Uses Emotion CSS-in-JS which has runtime overhead. Optional CSS variables mode reduces runtime but doesn't eliminate it

lazy-loading.comment: Components can be lazy loaded using React.lazy() and dynamic imports

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent documentation: comprehensive API references, interactive examples, guides, recipes, TypeScript support, SSR/RSC guides, migration paths. Known for 'unrivaled documentation'

interactive-docs.comment: Live component playground/sandbox in documentation with editable code examples

storybook.comment: No official public Storybook. Documentation includes extensive interactive examples but not via Storybook format

figma-kit.comment: Official Figma kit available for $79. Also available for Adobe XD ($69) and Sketch ($69)

cli-generator.comment: No CLI tool for scaffolding. Next.js 13.5+ includes automatic optimization via optimizePackageImports. ESLint config available to enforce path imports

commercial-support.comment: MUI X Pro and Premium tiers offer commercial licenses and technical support for enterprise applications

style-override.comment: Multiple override methods: sx prop (recommended), styled() API, className, inline style prop, theme overrides, CSS variables, GlobalStyles component

compound-components.comment: Extensive use of compound components: Accordion.Summary/Details/Actions, List.Item/ItemButton/ItemIcon/ItemText, Table.Head/Body/Row/Cell, etc.

render-props.comment: Limited use of render props pattern. Primarily uses children, slots, and compound components

slots.comment: Components support slots pattern for content injection and customization, particularly newer components adopting Base UI structure

unstyled-mode.comment: Base UI (separate package, formerly @mui/base) provides unstyled/headless versions of components with accessibility and behavior only
